product.one_line_description: Firecrawl is an API for web crawling, scraping, search, and interaction, providing AI-ready content and structured data for AI agents and builders.

authentication.auth_summary: Authentication is primarily done via an API key, which is included in an Authorization header as a Bearer token. For MCP servers, OAuth access tokens can also be used and are forwarded as Bearer tokens. A keyless free tier is available for certain API functionalities.

developer_access.access_notes: Developers can sign up and obtain an API key instantly without human approval from the Firecrawl website. A keyless free tier is available for `scrape`, `search`, and `interact` tools, albeit with rate limits. An API key or OAuth is preferred for unlocking the full toolset and higher rate limits.

api_surface.api_summary: Firecrawl provides a comprehensive API surface with features for web scraping (markdown, HTML, JSON, branding), crawling entire websites, mapping URLs, web search, interactive browser automation (click, fill forms), structured data extraction via LLMs, autonomous web research agents, and recurring page monitoring with diffing. It offers REST endpoints, Python and Node.js SDKs, a CLI, and supports webhooks for asynchronous event delivery. It also integrates with AI tools via an official Model Context Protocol (MCP) server.

mcp.mcp_summary: Firecrawl provides an official Model Context Protocol (MCP) server, designed to integrate powerful web scraping and search capabilities with MCP-compatible AI agents and LLM clients like Cursor, Claude, Windsurf, and VS Code. The MCP server supports search, scrape, and interact functions in a keyless free tier (rate-limited), with an API key or OAuth unlocking the full tool set including crawl, map, agent, and extract features.

buildability.verdict_reasoning: Self-serve developer access is verified with a broad/moderate documented public API.

evidence_pool.evidence_snippet: A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that brings Firecrawl to MCP-compatible AI agents — search, scrape, and interact with the live web for clean, agent-ready context.

For authentication, it’s required to include an Authorization header. The header should contain `Bearer fc-123456789`, where `fc-123456789` represents your API Key.
```
Authorization: Bearer fc-123456789
```
api_key="fc-YOUR-API-KEY"

evidence_pool.evidence_snippet: Hosted Firecrawl can issue OAuth **access tokens** (`fco_…`) via the authorization server on [firecrawl.dev](https://firecrawl.dev). This MCP server forwards whichever credential it resolves to the Firecrawl API as `Authorization: Bearer …`.

No API key needed to get started — add one for higher rate limits:
# api_key="fc-YOUR-API-KEY",

evidence_pool.source_title: GitHub - firecrawl/firecrawl-mcp-server: 🔥 Official Firecrawl MCP Server - Adds powerful web scraping and search to Cursor, Claude and any other LLM clients. · GitHub
evidence_pool.evidence_snippet: A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that brings [Firecrawl](https://github.com/firecrawl/firecrawl) to MCP-compatible AI agents — search, scrape, and interact with the live web for clean, agent-ready context.

## Scrape
Extract content from any webpage in markdown or json format.

## Crawl
Crawl entire websites, extract their content and metadata.

## Map
Get a complete list of URLs from any website quickly and reliably.

## Search
Search the web and get full page content in any format.

## Extract
Extract structured data from entire webpages using natural language.

## What can Firecrawl do?

## Search
Search the web and get full page content from results

## Scrape
Extract content from any URL as markdown, HTML, or structured JSON

## Interact
Continue working with any scraped page — click, fill forms, extract dynamic content

evidence_pool.source_title: GitHub - firecrawl/firecrawl-mcp-server: 🔥 Official Firecrawl MCP Server - Adds powerful web scraping and search to Cursor, Claude and any other LLM clients. · GitHub
evidence_pool.evidence_snippet:
## Available Tools
### 1. Scrape Tool (`firecrawl_scrape`)
### 2. Map Tool (`firecrawl_map`)
### 3. Search Tool (`firecrawl_search`)
### 3b. Search Feedback Tool (`firecrawl_search_feedback`)
### 3c. Generic Feedback Tool (`firecrawl_feedback`)
### 4. Crawl Tool (`firecrawl_crawl`)
### 5. Check Crawl Status (`firecrawl_check_crawl_status`)
### 6. Parse Tool (`firecrawl_parse`)
### 7. Extract Tool (`firecrawl_extract`)
### 8. Agent Tool (`firecrawl_agent`)
### 9. Check Agent Status (`firecrawl_agent_status`)
### 10. Interact Tool (`firecrawl_interact`)
### 11. Stop Interact Tool (`firecrawl_interact_stop`)
### 12. Research Tools (`firecrawl_research_*`)
### 13. Monitor Tools (`firecrawl_monitor_*`)
Or use the [MCP Server](/mcp-server) to connect Firecrawl directly to Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code, and other AI tools.

The MCP server supports search, scrape, and interact functions in a keyless free tier (rate-limited), with an API key or OAuth unlocking the full tool set including crawl, map, agent, and extract features.
evidence_pool.evidence_snippet:
Use Firecrawl with AI agents (recommended)
The Firecrawl skills are the fastest way for agents to discover and use Firecrawl. Without them, your agent will not know Firecrawl is available.
```
npx -y firecrawl-cli@latest init --all --browser
```
Restart your agent after installing the skills. See [Skills + CLI](/sdks/cli) for the full setup.

Or use the [MCP Server](/mcp-server) to connect Firecrawl directly to Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code, and other AI tools.

The MCP server supports search, scrape, and interact functions in a keyless free tier (rate-limited), with an API key or OAuth unlocking the full tool set including crawl, map, agent, and extract features.
evidence_pool.source_title: GitHub - firecrawl/firecrawl-mcp-server: 🔥 Official Firecrawl MCP Server - Adds powerful web scraping and search to Cursor, Claude and any other LLM clients. · GitHub
evidence_pool.evidence_snippet:
A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that brings [Firecrawl](https://github.com/firecrawl/firecrawl) to MCP-compatible AI agents — search, scrape, and interact with the live web for clean, agent-ready context.

## Installation
### Hosted MCP (keyless free tier)

Connect to the remote hosted server with no setup:
```
https://mcp.firecrawl.dev/v2/mcp
```
On the keyless free tier, `scrape`, `search`, and `interact` work without an API key (rate-limited). Other tools such as `crawl`, `map`, `agent`, and `extract` still need a key.

Prefer an API key or OAuth whenever the human can sign up. It unlocks the full tool set and higher limits. With a key, use:
```
https://mcp.firecrawl.dev/{FIRECRAWL_API_KEY}/v2/mcp
```

See the [MCP server docs](https://docs.firecrawl.dev/mcp-server) and the [agent onboarding guide](https://www.firecrawl.dev/agent-onboarding/SKILL.md) for setup details.

### Running on Cursor
Configuring Cursor 🖥️ Note: Requires Cursor version 0.45.6+ ...
### Running on Windsurf
Add this to your `./codeium/windsurf/model_config.json`: ...
### Running on VS Code
For one-click installation, click one of the install buttons below...
### Usage with Claude Desktop
Add this to your `claude_desktop_config.json`: ...
